description: Fix a race condition in the shared atomicWrite utility where concurrent writes to the same file use a predictable temp file path, causing data corruption.

userStories.title: Fix atomicWrite to use unique temp file paths
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want atomicWrite to use unique temporary file names so that concurrent writes to the same file do not corrupt each other's data.
acceptanceCriteria: atomicWrite uses a unique temp file path (e.g., including process.pid and a random suffix or crypto.randomUUID())
Two concurrent atomicWrite calls to the same file do not interfere with each other's temp files
The temp file is still cleaned up on failure (the catch block at line 81 must use the new unique path)
Existing unit tests in cross-platform.test.ts pass
A new test verifies that concurrent atomicWrite calls do not corrupt data
userStories.notes:
File: platform/shared/src/cross-platform.ts, line 63.

The bug: `const tmpPath = filePath + '.tmp'` is deterministic. If two concurrent calls write to the same `filePath`, they both write to `filePath.tmp`, causing a race:
1. Caller A writes content-A to file.tmp
2. Caller B overwrites file.tmp with content-B
3. Caller A renames file.tmp → file (now has content-B!)
4. Caller B's rename succeeds or fails depending on timing

This can happen when the MCP server's config write mutex serializes within one process, but two separate processes (e.g., CLI + server, or two CLI invocations) write simultaneously.

Fix: Use a unique temp file name. The simplest approach:
```typescript
const tmpPath = `${filePath}.tmp.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}.${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
```

Or if crypto is already imported:
```typescript
import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto';
const tmpPath = `${filePath}.tmp.${randomUUID()}`;
```

Check if crypto is already imported in this file. If not, use the process.pid + Date.now() + Math.random() approach to avoid adding a new import.

Make sure the cleanup in the catch block (line 81) references the same `tmpPath` variable — since it's a `const` in the same scope, this should already work correctly.
